target.title: feat(agents): generalized native compaction ownership for CLI backends
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `ownsNativeCompaction` capability to `CliBackendPlugin` so backends that manage their own transcript compaction can declare it and OpenClaw defers instead of fighting or hard-failing the turn
- Claude-cli is the first adopter: Claude Code compacts its own transcript internally, so OpenClaw returns a no-op from the compaction path
- Codex's existing native-harness compaction path is unchanged (agentHarnessId match still routes to the harness endpoint)
- Generalizes the partial fix in #87785 (codex-scoped) to a capability any CLI backend can opt into

## Problem

`claude-cli` runs Claude Code, which owns its own transcript and auto-compacts when context fills. OpenClaw does not know this, so the safeguard summarizer fires and fights Claude's compaction, hard-failing the turn.

The current workaround is `contextTokens: 1_000_000` so the safeguard never fires. This PR removes the need for that workaround.

## Behavior proof

Production compaction failure captured from a live claude-cli session (session `39bd8759`, 2026-05-16T21:59:57Z):

```
[assistant] [provider: openclaw, model: delivery-mirror]
Context limit exceeded during compaction. I've reset our conversation
to start fresh - please try again.

To prevent this, increase your compaction buffer by setting
`agents.defaults.compaction.reserveTokensFloor` to 20000 or higher
in your config.
```

This is the exact failure mode this PR prevents: OpenClaw's safeguard summarizer fires on a claude-cli session that already manages its own compaction, exceeds its budget fighting the backend's own compaction lifecycle, and force-resets the conversation. With `ownsNativeCompaction: true`, the lifecycle returns a no-op and the session continues normally.

The workaround currently deployed (`contextTokens: 1_000_000`) masks the issue by preventing the safeguard from ever firing. This PR fixes the root cause.

## Changes

**`src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts`** -- `ownsNativeCompaction?: boolean` on `CliBackendPlugin`

**`src/agents/cli-backends.ts`** -- Propagated through `ResolvedCliBackend`, `FallbackCliBackendPolicy`, and all 4 resolution paths

**`src/agents/command/cli-compaction.ts`** -- Added `resolveCliBackendConfig` to injectable deps. In `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle`, when `ownsNativeCompaction` is true and no native harness endpoint exists, returns a no-op early

**`extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts`** -- `ownsNativeCompaction: true` on claude-cli

## Scope

Only changes behavior for CLI backends that explicitly opt in via `ownsNativeCompaction: true`. Every other backend keeps the existing safeguard. Does not touch the embedded runner path.

## Not tested

- acpx / copilot backends (candidates for future adoption, not wired in this PR)
- Full e2e filling a context window with the fix applied (validated via unit tests covering the exact code path + production failure evidence above)

## Risk / mitigation

- **Risk:** A backend incorrectly declares ownership but does not actually compact -- context grows unbounded. **Mitigation:** Opt-in flag, not default; only set on claude-cli which is known to own compaction.
- **Risk:** Breaks existing codex path. **Mitigation:** `ownsNativeCompaction` early-out only fires when `isNativeHarnessCompactionSession` returns false; codex sessions with `agentHarnessId` still route to the existing native harness path. Verified by test.

## Test plan

- [x] Backend with `ownsNativeCompaction` and no harness endpoint skips compaction (no-op)
- [x] Backend without `ownsNativeCompaction` compacts normally via context engine
- [x] Backend with `ownsNativeCompaction` AND agentHarnessId uses existing native harness path
- [x] `resolveCliBackendConfig("claude-cli")` returns `ownsNativeCompaction: true`
- [x] All 30 existing cli-compaction tests pass (no regressions)
- [x] All 48 existing cli-backends tests pass (no regressions)

target.title: fix(config): skip state-dir dotenv values that are unresolved shell references
target.body:
Fixes #88274

## Summary

`readStateDirDotEnvVarsFromStateDir` accepted every non-empty value from `~/.openclaw/.env` without checking whether the value is an unresolved shell variable reference. `dotenv.parse` preserves these references literally — it does not expand `$VAR` or `${VAR}`. When a literal reference like `${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}` reaches the LaunchAgent/systemd wrapper, it is single-quoted and written to the service env file as-is, so the plugin receives the literal string `${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}` instead of the actual credential value.

The fix adds `isUnresolvedShellReference()` and skips values whose **entire content** is a recognised shell reference form:

- `$VAR_NAME` — simple variable reference (must start with a letter or underscore)
- `${VAR_NAME}` — brace-form variable reference
- `$(command)` — command substitution

Real credentials that merely **contain** a `$` (e.g. a password `abc$2!xyz`, a price `$100`, or a token `tok_$prod_v2`) are **not** skipped — the regex is anchored to the whole value and requires the variable name to start with a letter or underscore.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `parseStateDirDotEnvContent` passes literal shell references like `${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}` into the service env. After the fix, only values whose entire content is a recognised shell reference are skipped. Real credentials containing `$` in the middle or at the start of a dollar-number remain in the service env.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node.js v22.22.0, commit `d0fb8531e5ee9e137033ccbfd7341d8013347984`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
node --input-type=module -e "
import dotenv from 'dotenv';
function isRef(v) {
  return /^\\\$[A-Za-z_]\w*\$/.test(v) || /^\\\$\{[^}]+\}\$/.test(v) || /^\\\$\(.*\)\$/.test(v);
}
const cases = [
  [\"\\\${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}\", true],
  [\"\\\$SOME_VAR\", true],
  [\"abc\\\$2!xyz\", false],
  [\"sm_real_credential\", false],
];
for (const [v, expected] of cases)
  console.log((isRef(v)===expected?'OK':'FAIL'), JSON.stringify(v), '→', isRef(v)?'skip':'keep');
"
```

**Evidence after fix:**

Before — all values passed through regardless of shell references:

```
dotenv.parse result includes: SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_API_KEY = "${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}"
```

After — whole-value references skipped, real credentials kept:

```
$ node --input-type=module -e "..."
OK "${SUPERMEMORY_OPENCLAW_KEY}" → skip
OK "$SOME_VAR" → skip
OK "abc$2!xyz" → keep
OK "sm_real_credential" → keep
```

Regression tests (4/4, including a test that `$`-containing real credentials are not dropped):

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)
   Duration  488ms
```

**Observed result after fix:** `parseStateDirDotEnvContent` skips `$VAR` and `${VAR}` whole-value references. Real credentials that merely contain a `$` character are preserved. The service env no longer receives literal shell reference strings for affected values.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end gateway service installation and restart on macOS. The filtering logic is exercised by regression tests using real dotenv parsing and real temp directories.

## Summary
- Classifies the exact `ws` pre-handshake close error as a benign uncaught network exception.
- Keeps the fix centralized in `src/infra/unhandled-rejections.ts` so both gateway and CLI global handlers use the same classifier.
- Adds regression coverage for direct and wrapped/cause forms of the error.

Fixes #88257

## Verification
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/unhandled-rejections.test.ts -t 'treats raw pre-connect network uncaught exceptions as benign'`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/unhandled-rejections.test.ts`

Narrow file lint passed separately.

## Real behavior proof

```text
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module

```

Supplemental unit proof: `src/infra/unhandled-rejections.test.ts` passed 65/65 tests; the focused benign-classifier test also passed.

Observed result after fix: direct and wrapped/cause versions of the `ws` pre-handshake close message are classified as benign.

target.title: fix(codex): keep app-server continuation turns alive
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88196.
Fixes #88331.

Codex app-server turns can continue silently after OpenClaw returns a tool result or after Codex emits no-tool reasoning/commentary progress while it synthesizes the final assistant response. The old watchdog path applied the short `appServer.turnCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` window to those continuation states, so quiet synthesis could fail with `codex app-server attempt timed out` or `turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed`.

This change makes the continuation budgets consistent:

- extend both the completion idle watch and the attempt progress watchdog with `appServer.postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` after OpenClaw dynamic tool responses, native tool completions, raw `custom_tool_call_output` completions, and post-tool raw assistant progress
- use the existing post-reasoning reply guard after no-tool raw reasoning, reasoning item completion, or non-final commentary assistant progress
- preserve the short completion idle timeout for accepted turns that never produce tool, reasoning, or assistant progress
- cover buffered post-tool notifications replayed during `turn/start`
- update the Codex harness docs and plugin metadata to describe the broadened post-tool timeout contract

## Verification

- `pnpm format:docs -- docs/plugins/codex-harness.md docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md`
- `pnpm format -- extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notification-state.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-notifications.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-timeouts.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.turn-watches.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.codex-app-server-recovery.test.ts`
  - passed after final #88331 update: 2 Vitest shards, 64 tests
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
  - clean after final #88331 update, no accepted/actionable findings
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
- Dependency-surface scan for package files/lockfiles/patches returned no changed files.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server turns no longer fail the short turn-completion idle watchdog after tool/progress continuation while waiting for final assistant synthesis. Scheduled cron `agentTurn` runs now survive the same post-progress gap that reproduced #88196.

Real environment tested: Two isolated local canary Gateways with separate config/state/ports, Crabboxer Discord bot, `gpt-5.5`, `appServer.turnCompletionIdleTimeoutMs=3000`, and `appServer.postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs=90000`. Before proof used the `27ae826` release checkout that matches OpenClaw `2026.5.27`; after proof used this patched worktree. Both jobs were one-shot `schedule.kind=at` isolated cron `agentTurn`s that were auto-fired by the scheduler, announced to the Discord test channel, and asked to call the read-only OpenClaw `cron` tool with `action=status`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the patched Gateway canary, created scheduled job `1fe927d9-5ecf-46c3-baf9-8b0bc3742591` with `openclaw cron add --at +12s --keep-after-run --message <cron status tool prompt> --model openai-codex/gpt-5.5 --thinking high --light-context --announce --channel discord --to channel:<test-channel-id>`, waited with `openclaw cron runs --id 1fe927d9-5ecf-46c3-baf9-8b0bc3742591`, then inspected the session trajectory. For before proof, ran the same scheduled command and prompt against the `27ae826` canary as job `853e5847-1d55-4070-acd2-35ddf51d311f`. The final branch was also checked with focused Vitest, autoreview, `git diff --check`, and `pnpm build`.

Evidence after fix: Patched scheduled job `1fe927d9-5ecf-46c3-baf9-8b0bc3742591` auto-fired at `2026-05-30 14:34 UTC`, session `850e03ba-1e01-4ffa-88f6-ffa121d80eab`, recorded two `cron` tool calls (`tool.call` seq 4 and 6; `tool.result` seq 5 and 7), then `model.completed timedOut=false`, `session.ended status=success`, run status `ok`, delivery `delivered`, and final text containing `CRON_TRIGGER_88196_CRON_TOOL_OK enabled=true`. The `cron status` tool result only returned `{ "enabled": true }`; the prompt's extra `jobs` slot was not a tool-result field. Session-scoped timeout scan found no `turn.completion_idle_timeout`, no `codex app-server attempt timed out`, no `codex app-server turn idle timed out`, and no `Codex stopped before confirming` signature. The matching pre-fix scheduled job `853e5847-1d55-4070-acd2-35ddf51d311f` auto-fired at `2026-05-30 14:35 UTC`, session `e9c80cca-cb17-414b-8daf-834fb65f0626`, ended `status=error`, provider `openai-codex`, delivery `not-delivered`, and returned the exact #88196 error: `Codex stopped before confirming the turn was complete. The response may be incomplete; retry if needed.` Its trajectory recorded `turn.completion_idle_timeout idleMs=3002 timeoutMs=3000 lastActivityReason=notification:item/completed`, followed by `model.completed timedOut=true promptError="codex app-server attempt timed out"` and `session.ended promptError="codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed"`.

Copied live output / redacted runtime log excerpt:

```text
Pre-fix scheduled job 853e5847-1d55-4070-acd2-35ddf51d311f (27ae826):
status: error
provider: openai-codex
model: gpt-5.5
delivered: false
deliveryStatus: not-delivered
error: Codex stopped before confirming the turn was complete. The response may be incomplete; retry if needed.
trajectory: turn.completion_idle_timeout idleMs=3002 timeoutMs=3000 lastActivityReason=notification:item/completed
trajectory: model.completed timedOut=true promptError="codex app-server attempt timed out"
trajectory: session.ended status=error promptError="codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed"

Patched scheduled job 1fe927d9-5ecf-46c3-baf9-8b0bc3742591:
status: ok
model: gpt-5.5
delivered: true
deliveryStatus: delivered
session: 850e03ba-1e01-4ffa-88f6-ffa121d80eab
tool.call: cron {"action":"status"}
tool.result: {"enabled": true}
tool.call: cron {"action":"status","includeDisabled":true}
tool.result: {"enabled": true}
finalAssistantVisibleText: CRON_TRIGGER_88196_CRON_TOOL_OK enabled=true (cron status did not return a jobs field)
trajectory: model.completed timedOut=false promptError=null
trajectory: session.ended status=success timedOut=false promptError=null
session timeout scan: 0 matches for turn.completion_idle_timeout / codex app-server attempt timed out / turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed / Codex stopped before confirming

Additional patched live Discord post-tool proof for the shared app-server path:
Gateway-driven run 87d39cbb-e7ad-4267-bef5-405d3b3e45b3 returned CRABBOXER_LIVE_88196_OK after one bash tool call and delivered to Discord.
Inbound Discord run 3f1bde48-8f6a-448f-a4da-d5fd2749fd5a ran printf, returned CRABBOXER_INBOUND_88196_OK, and Discord API confirmed bot message 1510250835492339723.
```

Observed result after fix: The exact scheduler-triggered canary no longer hits the short idle guard. The patched run completed after tool results and delivered to Discord; the matching release checkout reproduced #88196's not-delivered cron error with the same app-server timeout signatures. Focused #88331 tests also showed no-tool reasoning/commentary continuation no longer trips the old short guard.

What was not tested: Full test suite and a post-fix live replay of the no-tool Discord image/avatar prompt from #88331. The cron scheduler path, Codex app-server continuation watchdog, OpenClaw `cron` tool handoff, and Discord delivery were tested live.

## Compatibility and Availability Notes

This intentionally broadens the existing advanced `appServer.postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` option. The option is now the post-tool continuation budget for both completion-idle and attempt-progress watchdogs after tool handoff, native tool completion, raw tool-output completion, or post-tool raw assistant progress. That is the intended upgrade contract because those states all mean Codex is past tool execution and OpenClaw is waiting for final assistant synthesis.

No-tool turns that emit reasoning or non-final commentary assistant progress now use the existing five-minute post-reasoning reply guard for both completion-idle and attempt-progress watches. That avoids interrupting active final-response synthesis while still preserving fast failure for accepted turns that never produce progress.

The availability tradeoff is intentional: a genuinely wedged post-tool or post-reasoning Codex turn can now occupy the session lane until the relevant continuation timeout instead of the shorter completion timeout. The default remains bounded at five minutes and only applies after tool, reasoning, or assistant progress.

Cron-specific proof now covers the ClawSweeper gap: the same one-shot isolated cron job auto-fired on the 27ae826 release checkout and reproduced #88196, then auto-fired on this patched worktree and completed after two cron tool calls with Discord delivery. Full suite and the #88331 no-tool live image replay remain untested.

target.title: fix(control-ui): respect chat history max chars config
target.body:
## Summary

This is the intentionally small fix for the Control UI/WebChat truncation bug tracked in #87897.

Control UI was sending every `chat.history` request with an explicit `maxChars: 4000`. The gateway correctly gives an RPC `maxChars` parameter precedence over `gateway.webchat.chatHistoryMaxChars`, so the documented/user-configured setting was ignored whenever history was loaded through Control UI.

This removes the hardcoded Control UI `maxChars` override so `chat.history` can fall back to the gateway's configured/default limit.

Fixes #87897.

## Notes

This is deliberately not the larger full-message-reader work from #84651/#84670. That UI can still be useful later, but it should not block the minimal config-precedence fix: if a user explicitly sets `gateway.webchat.chatHistoryMaxChars`, Control UI should respect it.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Control UI/WebChat history reload should respect `gateway.webchat.chatHistoryMaxChars` instead of always forcing a 4,000-character `chat.history` cap from the client.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw gateway from this PR branch (`toruviel/fix-control-ui-chat-history-maxchars`) on macOS arm64, started with `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr-proof/config.json`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr-proof/state`, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19031`. Config set `gateway.webchat.chatHistoryMaxChars = 64000`; auth was disabled only in this throwaway local proof profile and Control UI device auth was disabled only to allow a scripted local Control UI WebSocket proof client.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Started the local gateway from the PR checkout, seeded the main session transcript with one assistant message containing 500 sentinel lines (~47k chars), then used a small Control UI WebSocket proof client to call `chat.history` twice: once as Control UI now does after this PR (`{ sessionKey, limit: 100 }`, no client `maxChars`) and once with the old explicit `{ maxChars: 4000 }` override for comparison.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the live local gateway/proof client:

```text
sessionKey=agent:main:main
config_chatHistoryMaxChars=64000
noRpcMaxChars_len=47733
noRpcMaxChars_has_SENTINEL_001=true
noRpcMaxChars_has_SENTINEL_050=true
noRpcMaxChars_has_SENTINEL_400=true
noRpcMaxChars_has_SENTINEL_500=true
noRpcMaxChars_has_truncated=false
rpcMax4000_len=4296
rpcMax4000_has_SENTINEL_001=true
rpcMax4000_has_SENTINEL_050=false
rpcMax4000_has_SENTINEL_400=false
rpcMax4000_has_SENTINEL_500=false
rpcMax4000_has_truncated=true
```

- **Observed result after fix**: With no RPC `maxChars`, the live gateway returned the full seeded long history under the configured 64k cap and included sentinel lines through `SENTINEL-500` with no truncation marker. With the old explicit `maxChars: 4000` override, the same live gateway response was truncated around 4.3k JSON chars and only included the early sentinel lines. This confirms the PR restores the documented config/default path.
- **What was not tested**: A screenshot/recording of the browser-rendered bubble was not captured because the proof focuses on the exact Control UI request/projection boundary that caused the bug. Unit/UI tests and build were run separately.

## Testing

- `../node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts src/ui/app-chat.test.ts` from `ui/`
- `../node_modules/.bin/vite build` from `ui/`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(plugins): block untrusted workspace setup-only channel loads
target.body:
## Summary - Problem: latest `main` already keeps execution-facing channel setup discovery on the trusted catalog path, but lower-level setup-only scoped plugin loads could still import and execute disabled workspace channel plugins when callers passed explicit `onlyPluginIds`. - What changed: this PR restores the lower-level security boundary from #64154 by blocking disabled workspace channel plugins before setup-only import, adds a registry backstop that rejects channel registration from disabled workspace plugins, and ports the complementary #85774 guard so a broken trusted setup-entry channel reports a diagnostic instead of aborting sibling plugin loading. - What latest `main` already kept from #64154: `channels add`, onboarding/setup resolution, and setup discovery now use trust-aware catalog helpers; untrusted workspace shadows fall back to bundled/non-workspace entries; workspace-only candidates remain visible only in the setup wizard discovery bucket; the raw catalog listing is kept behind an internal/deprecated helper. - Remaining work before merge: no known #64154 hardening gap remains in code after this PR. The remaining gates are CI plus any maintainer-requested live external-plugin proof. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Replaces/supersedes #64154 - Includes the valuable complementary setup-entry error-isolation fix from #85774 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Current Main vs This PR The #64154 design had two layers: 1. Catalog callers should resolve channels through trusted helpers so setup/add flows do not select untrusted workspace shadows. 2. The loader/registry should enforce the same trust boundary before any plugin module can execute, so lower-level callers cannot bypass the catalog layer. Latest `main` still preserves the first layer. `channels add`, setup/onboarding, and channel setup plugin resolution use `listTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntries` / `getTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntry`; the setup wizard's installable display can still show workspace-only candidates through the lenient discovery helper without treating them as executable/installed. This PR fills the second layer. It prevents explicit setup-only scoped loads from importing disabled workspace channel plugins, rejects disabled workspace channel registrations at the registry boundary, and keeps trusted workspace overrides working. It also ports #85774's try/catch around `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)`, which addresses a related but distinct resilience gap: trusted setup-entry plugins are allowed to load, but one malformed registration should not stop healthy sibling plugins from loading. ## Diagram ```mermaid graph TD SOURCES["Plugin source directories"] --> MANIFEST["Manifest registry
(pluginId + origin + channels)"] MANIFEST -->|"setup / onboard / channels add"| TRUSTED["Trusted catalog helpers
strict execution list
untrusted workspace shadows fall back"] MANIFEST -->|"setup wizard display"| DISCOVERY["Setup discovery helper
lenient display list
workspace-only candidates visible"] MANIFEST -->|"gateway or explicit scoped setup load"| LOADER["loadOpenClawPlugins()"] TRUSTED --> SETUP["setup/add snapshot request"] DISCOVERY --> UI["installable UI bucket only
not an execution decision"] SETUP --> LOADER LOADER --> GATE{"This PR:
workspace channel plugin enabled?"} GATE -->|"disabled workspace"| BLOCK["reject before setup-only import

target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file via Crabbox Docker using the command above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and a slow setup that still passes the full configured timeout through to the provider request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed, provider-request calls still received `timeoutMs: 1000` after a mocked `400ms` setup delay, and timeout messages now distinguish `setup` vs `request`.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce the image runtime-loading latency itself.

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression

## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(plugins): block untrusted workspace setup-only channel loads
target.body:
## Summary - Problem: latest `main` already keeps execution-facing channel setup discovery on the trusted catalog path, but lower-level setup-only scoped plugin loads could still import and execute disabled workspace channel plugins when callers passed explicit `onlyPluginIds`. - What changed: this PR restores the lower-level security boundary from #64154 by blocking disabled workspace channel plugins before setup-only import, adds a registry backstop that rejects channel registration from disabled workspace plugins, and ports the complementary #85774 guard so a broken trusted setup-entry channel reports a diagnostic instead of aborting sibling plugin loading. - What latest `main` already kept from #64154: `channels add`, onboarding/setup resolution, and setup discovery now use trust-aware catalog helpers; untrusted workspace shadows fall back to bundled/non-workspace entries; workspace-only candidates remain visible only in the setup wizard discovery bucket; the raw catalog listing is kept behind an internal/deprecated helper. - Remaining work before merge: no known #64154 hardening gap remains in code after this PR. The remaining gates are CI plus any maintainer-requested live external-plugin proof. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Replaces/supersedes #64154 - Includes the valuable complementary setup-entry error-isolation fix from #85774 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Current Main vs This PR The #64154 design had two layers: 1. Catalog callers should resolve channels through trusted helpers so setup/add flows do not select untrusted workspace shadows. 2. The loader/registry should enforce the same trust boundary before any plugin module can execute, so lower-level callers cannot bypass the catalog layer. Latest `main` still preserves the first layer. `channels add`, setup/onboarding, and channel setup plugin resolution use `listTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntries` / `getTrustedChannelPluginCatalogEntry`; the setup wizard's installable display can still show workspace-only candidates through the lenient discovery helper without treating them as executable/installed. This PR fills the second layer. It prevents explicit setup-only scoped loads from importing disabled workspace channel plugins, rejects disabled workspace channel registrations at the registry boundary, and keeps trusted workspace overrides working. It also ports #85774's try/catch around `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)`, which addresses a related but distinct resilience gap: trusted setup-entry plugins are allowed to load, but one malformed registration should not stop healthy sibling plugins from loading. ## Diagram ```mermaid graph TD SOURCES["Plugin source directories"] --> MANIFEST["Manifest registry
(pluginId + origin + channels)"] MANIFEST -->|"setup / onboard / channels add"| TRUSTED["Trusted catalog helpers
strict execution list
untrusted workspace shadows fall back"] MANIFEST -->|"setup wizard display"| DISCOVERY["Setup discovery helper
lenient display list
workspace-only candidates visible"] MANIFEST -->|"gateway or explicit scoped setup load"| LOADER["loadOpenClawPlugins()"] TRUSTED --> SETUP["setup/add snapshot request"] DISCOVERY --> UI["installable UI bucket only
not an execution decision"] SETUP --> LOADER LOADER --> GATE{"This PR:
workspace channel plugin enabled?"} GATE -->|"disabled workspace"| BLOCK["reject before setup-only import
no plugin code executes"] GATE -->|"trusted workspace or non-workspace"| IMPORT["import setup/runtime entry"] IMPORT --> REGISTER{"This PR + #85774:
registerChannel succeeds?"} REGISTER -->|"yes"| REGISTRY["snapshot/runtime registry"] REGISTER -->|"throws"| DIAG["record plugin diagnostic
continue loading siblings"] REGISTRY --> SNAPUSE["validateInput / applyAccountConfig
write config / lifecycle callbacks"] REGISTRY --> RUNTIME["gateway/runtime channel lookup"] BLOCK:::fixed GATE:::fixed DIAG:::fixed TRUSTED:::kept DISCOVERY:::display UI:::display classDef fixed fill:#d4edda,stroke:#28a745,stroke-width:2px classDef kept fill:#d9edf7,stroke:#31708f,stroke-width:2px classDef display fill:#fff3cd,stroke:#ffc107,stroke-width:2px ``` The important boundary is now shared: catalog helpers decide which channel catalog entries are trusted for setup/add flows, and the loader/registry enforce the same rule before runtime code can execute. If a trusted setup-entry channel still fails during registration, the error is isolated to that plugin and sibling plugins continue loading. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: untrusted workspace channel plugins should not execute during setup-only scoped loads; disabled workspace channel registrations should be rejected at registry assembly; trusted setup-entry channel registration failures should not abort sibling plugin loading. - Real environment tested: local Linux repo checkout rebased on `upstream/main` `717003aaffec3d41792424079a52beaf2bd92a5c`; PR head `55811ad9edce27d49decb99c7564fba5c4138fb3`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: - `pnpm docs:list` - `pnpm test src/commands/onboard-channels.e2e.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - `pnpm test src/plugins/loader.test.ts src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/channel-plugin-resolution.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/discovery.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/workspace-shadow-bypass.test.ts src/commands/channel-setup/plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/channels.add.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - `pnpm build` - `pnpm check:changed` - Evidence after fix: `src/commands/onboard-channels.e2e.test.ts` passes 16/16 after exposing `listRawChannelPluginCatalogEntries` through the same mock/actual fallback as the old catalog helper; the focused loader/registry/catalog/channel setup tests pass after the rebase; `pnpm build` and the final `pnpm check:changed` pass; `CHANGELOG.md` and stale transcripts tool-display metadata are no longer in the final branch diff. - Observed result after fix: disabled workspace shadows no longer execute through scoped setup-only loads; trusted workspace overrides remain available; disabled workspace channel registrations are rejected as a registry backstop; malformed trusted setup-entry channel registration records a diagnostic and sibling plugins keep loading. - What was not tested: live channel setup against a real external workspace plugin outside the loader test harness. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the same minimal setup-only repro failed on latest `origin/main` before this branch because the marker file was created during scoped setup-only load. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: the scoped setup-only loader path treated explicit `onlyPluginIds` as sufficient to load a disabled workspace channel plugin, so workspace trust was bypassed below the trusted-catalog call sites. - Missing detection / guardrail: registry assembly still accepted channel registration from disabled workspace plugins if a lower-level caller imported one. - Complementary #85774 root cause: the setup-entry loader loop caught surrounding registration failures, but `api.registerChannel(mergedSetupPlugin)` itself was unguarded, so one throwing trusted channel registration could abort sibling plugin loading. - Contributing context: latest `main` already absorbed the higher-level `channels add` / trusted-catalog call-site fixes from #64154, but not this lower-level loader/registry backstop or the setup-entry error-isolation guard. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [ ] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: `src/plugins/loader.test.ts`, `src/plugins/registry.channel-guard.test.ts`, `src/commands/channel-setup/trusted-catalog.test.ts`, and existing channel setup/add tests. - Scenario the test should lock in: explicit scoped setup-only loads must reject disabled workspace channel plugins but keep trusted workspace channel plugins available; registry assembly must reject disabled workspace channel registrations; trusted setup-entry registration errors must be recorded without aborting healthy sibling plugins. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bugs live at loader/registry/catalog boundaries and require real plugin import behavior plus registration-mode assertions. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): `only setup-loads a disabled channel plugin when the caller scopes to the selected plugin` still covers the non-workspace path. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - Untrusted workspace channel shadows no longer execute during setup-only scoped loads. - Trusted workspace channel plugins remain available to setup-time scoped loading. - A broken trusted setup-entry channel registration now surfaces as a plugin diagnostic instead of stopping sibling plugin loading. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux - Runtime/container: Node 22 local repo checkout - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): plugin loader / channel plugin registry / channel setup catalog - Relevant config (redacted): workspace plugins enabled; untrusted plugin omitted from allowlist ### Steps 1. Create a workspace channel plugin fixture with `channels` metadata and a side-effect marker write in `register()`. 2. Load it through `loadOpenClawPlugins({ includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true, forceSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true, onlyPluginIds: [...] })`. 3. Compare disabled workspace behavior against trusted workspace behavior. 4. Load a setup-entry channel whose channel config getter throws during `registerChannel` alongside a healthy sibling plugin. ### Expected - Disabled workspace channel plugin stays disabled and does not execute. - Trusted workspace channel plugin remains available to setup-only loading. - A throwing trusted setup-entry channel records a diagnostic and does not prevent sibling plugins from loading. ### Actual - This branch matches the expected behavior. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets in test output - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) - Verified scenarios: untrusted/trusted workspace scoped setup-only regressions, disabled workspace registry guard, trusted-catalog filtering semantics, onboarding channel setup e2e catalog mock compatibility, `channels add` workspace shadow fallback, #85774 setup-entry `registerChannel` error isolation, `pnpm build`, and final `pnpm check:changed`. - Edge cases checked: trusted workspace override still loads in setup-only mode; non-workspace disabled plugin behavior stays unchanged; setup wizard discovery still keeps workspace-only install candidates visible without treating them as installed/executable. - What you did **not** verify: a live end-to-end channel setup outside the loader test harness. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) No. This is an intentional security hardening break for disabled workspace channel plugins that were previously reachable through setup-only scoped loads. - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No automatic migration. Operators who intentionally develop or run a workspace/linked channel plugin must explicitly enable/trust that plugin before setup-time code can run. - If yes, exact upgrade steps: use the normal plugin enable/trust flow for the workspace plugin before running channel setup. The behavior is documented in `docs/cli/plugins.md`. ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: a trusted workspace plugin could accidentally get blocked if the trust check were too broad. - Mitigation: regression coverage explicitly keeps the trusted workspace scoped-load path green. - Risk: setup discovery could accidentally hide workspace-only install candidates. - Mitigation: trusted-catalog and discovery tests keep the strict execution list separate from the lenient setup wizard display list. - Risk: an existing disabled workspace channel plugin that relied on setup-only execution now fails closed. - Mitigation: this is the intended security behavior; the docs now state that workspace-origin plugins are not imported or executed until explicitly enabled/trusted. ### Proof 1: Pre-Fix Reproduction on origin/main - Behavior addressed: Untrusted workspace channel plugin (evi1-channel-probe) executes its register() function via the setup-only scoped load path when includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins: true is passed to loadOpenClawPlugins, bypassing the trust boundary. - Real environment tested: Crabbox docker, provider=docker, container=391883b7652bcb464c523f64ddcabb9bcad47f92e434737b7cc5391f7bc0922f, run=cbx_6dadb5d505eb, checkout SHA=ead847f6065ac36031f9e7a95dd1ee90f171f79c (origin/main) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: (1) `git fetch --depth=1 origin main && git checkout FETCH_HEAD` inside Crabbox container; (2) `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile && pnpm build`; (3) Deploy evi1-channel-probe plugin to /tmp/proof1-workspace/.openclaw/extensions/evi1-channel-probe/; (4) Confirm /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence absent; (5) Run `node probe-untrusted.mjs` with onlyPluginIds=["evi1-channel-probe"], includeSetupOnlyChannelPlugins=true, activate=false, cache=false; (6) Check /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence existence. - Evidence after fix: Marker file /tmp/openclaw-marker-evidence CREATED with content "evi1-probe-executed\n". target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- What was not tested: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities

- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
